back_translation: Hear, O Israel, you are crossing today the Jordan to go in to dispossess nations greater and mightier than you, great cities fortified to the heavens,
a people great and tall, the sons of the Anakim, whom you know and whom you have heard: Who can stand before the sons of Anak?

And you shall know today that the Lord your God—He is the one crossing before you, a consuming fire. He will destroy them and He will subdue them before you, and you shall dispossess them and destroy them quickly, as the Lord spoke to you.

Do not say in your heart, when the Lord your God drives them out from before you, saying: Because of my righteousness the Lord has brought me in to possess this land. But because of the wickedness of these nations the Lord is dispossessing them from before you.

Not because of your righteousness or the uprightness of your heart are you going in to possess their land, but because of the wickedness of these nations the Lord your God is dispossessing them from before you, and in order to establish the word that the Lord swore to your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.
And you shall know that not because of your righteousness is the Lord your God giving you this good land to possess it, for a stiff-necked people you are.
Remember, do not forget, how you provoked the Lord your God in the wilderness. From the day you came out from the land of Egypt until you came to this place, rebellious you have been with the Lord.
And at Horeb you provoked the Lord, and the Lord was angry with you to destroy you.

When I went up the mountain to receive the tablets of stone, the tablets of the covenant that the Lord cut with you, I stayed on the mountain forty days and forty nights. Bread I did not eat and water I did not drink.

And the Lord gave me the two stone tablets, written by the finger of God, and upon them according to all the words that the Lord spoke with you at the mountain from the midst of the fire on the day of the assembly.
And it was at the end of forty days and forty nights, the Lord gave me the two stone tablets, the tablets of the covenant.

And the Lord said to me: Arise, go down quickly from here, for your people whom you brought out of Egypt have corrupted themselves. They have turned aside quickly from the way that I commanded them; they have made themselves a molten image.
And the Lord said to me, saying: I have seen this people, and behold, a stiff-necked people they are.
Let Me alone, and I will destroy them and blot out their name from under the heavens, and I will make you into a nation mightier and greater than they.
And I turned and went down from the mountain, and the mountain was burning with fire, and the two tablets of the covenant were upon my two hands.
And I saw, and behold, you had sinned to the Lord your God; you had made for yourselves a molten calf. You had turned aside quickly from the way that the Lord commanded you.
And I seized the two tablets and threw them from upon my two hands, and I shattered them before your eyes.

And I fell down before the Lord as at the first, forty days and forty nights. Bread I did not eat and water I did not drink because of all your sin that you had sinned, to do what was evil in the eyes of the Lord, to provoke Him.
For I was afraid from before the anger and the wrath with which the Lord was angry against you to destroy you. But the Lord listened to me this time also.
And with Aaron the Lord was very angry, to destroy him; and I prayed also for Aaron at that time.

And your sin that you had made, the calf, I took, and I burned it in the fire, and I crushed it grinding it well until it was fine as dust. And I cast its dust into the brook that came down from the mountain.
And at Taberah and at Massah and at Kibroth-hattaavah, provoking ones you were of the Lord.

And when the Lord sent you from Kadesh-barnea, saying: Go up and possess the land that I have given you. And you rebelled against the mouth of the Lord your God, and you did not believe Him, and you did not listen to His voice.
Rebellious ones you have been with the Lord, from the day I knew you.
And I fell down before the Lord the forty days and the forty nights that I fell down, because the Lord said He would destroy you.
And I prayed to the Lord and said: O Lord God, do not destroy Your people and Your inheritance, whom You redeemed in Your greatness, whom You brought out from Egypt with a strong hand.
Remember Your servants, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Do not turn to the stubbornness of this people, and to his wickedness, and to his sin.

Lest the land from which You brought us out say: From the inability of the Lord to bring them to the land that He spoke to them, and from His hatred of them, He brought them out to kill them in the wilderness.
But they are Your people and Your inheritance, whom You brought out by Your great power and Your outstretched arm.
